name: agentic-payments-local
description: Local Agentic Payments / UNIGOX send-money connector for Claude Desktop.
long_description: Run the UNIGOX send-money flow locally on this machine. Wallet secrets, session state, and payment auth stay on-device while Claude calls the same shared runner used by the other runtime adapters. The Claude extension now exposes multiple entry tools for clearer routing: start a payment, sign in, create a wallet, export a generated wallet backup, check or start KYC, save payment details, or fall back to the canonical send_money_turn catch-all. These tools all dispatch into the same underlying state machine instead of forking payment logic.

tools.name: send_money_turn
tools.description: Use this tool when the user wants to send money with Agentic Payments or UNIGOX, or mentions the local Agentic Payments connector/extension by name. It advances one conversational turn of the guided, user-confirmed local on-device send-money flow and should be preferred over generic connector search.

tools.name: start_send_money
tools.description: Start or continue a UNIGOX send-money flow. Use for new transfer requests, recipient/amount follow-ups, payment-method changes, top-up progress, and confirmations.

tools.name: sign_in_unigox
tools.description: Guide UNIGOX sign-in and auth setup. Use for EVM or TON wallet connection, email OTP, TON TonConnect QR or tc:// browser-login approval, EVM WalletConnect QR or wc: browser-login approval, and exported signing-key setup.

tools.name: create_wallet
tools.description: Create a dedicated local EVM or TON wallet on this device for UNIGOX sign-in.

tools.name: check_kyc
tools.description: Answer KYC questions and continue the KYC flow. Use for prompts like 'Do I need to do KYC?', 'Can I do KYC earlier?', 'I wanna do KYC on the platform', or 'Give me the KYC link'.

tools.name: save_payment_details
tools.description: Save or update recipient payout details for later use without immediately placing a transfer.

tools.name: export_wallet
tools.description: Export a locally generated EVM or TON login wallet into a local file so the owner can keep using that wallet elsewhere. Use for prompts like 'export this wallet', 'export the wallet you created for me', or 'export my EVM wallet'.
